name: Local-Density Approximation (LDA) exchange-correlation functional
principles: homogeneous electron gas as the local benchmark
conventions: rho(r) is the electronic density at position r
epsilon_xc^hom(rho) is the per-electron exchange-correlation energy of the uniform electron gas at density rho — known to high accuracy from quantum Monte Carlo (Ceperley-Alder 1980)
exchange part is analytic: epsilon_x^hom = -(3/4)(3 rho/pi)^(1/3) (Dirac exchange)
correlation part fitted to QMC data — Perdew-Zunger 1981 and Vosko-Wilk-Nusair 1980 are common parametrisations
spin-polarised generalisation: LSDA — separate functional of rho_up(r) and rho_down(r)
expectedLimits: high-density limit rho -> infinity: epsilon_x dominates, correlation vanishes (Hartree-Fock-like)
low-density limit rho -> 0+: Wigner crystal regime, strong correlation
slowly-varying density: LDA is exact for the uniform electron gas, accurate for solids with delocalised electrons (sp-bonded metals, simple semiconductors)
LDA underestimates band gaps by 30-50% (the band-gap problem) — fundamental limitation of any local functional
LDA over-binds molecules and over-estimates cohesive energies — partially fixed by GGA / hybrid functionals
references: Kohn & Sham (1965) — Self-Consistent Equations Including Exchange and Correlation Effects
Ceperley & Alder (1980) — Ground State of the Electron Gas by a Stochastic Method
Perdew & Zunger (1981) — Self-interaction correction to density-functional approximations
Martin — Electronic Structure: Basic Theory and Practical Methods, ch.7-9
